title: chore: commit PR #35 cycle script + audit + fix True-vs-true bug
actions: captured pre_reviews_rule_pr37.json (live rule at cycle start)
verified pre-conditions: 1 open PR, MERGEABLE, OPEN
PUT step ran cleanly: built PUT body from canonical via python (no jq dependency), PUT succeeded
drift check: approvals=1, contexts=[], strict=true (verified via LIVE_STRICT_PYTHON for shell-side check, emitted as JSON-valid via json.dumps in LIVE_STRICT)
notes: The Option 3 cycle for PR #37 is the first cycle whose audit JSON is valid out of the box. The True-vs-true bug (where the heredoc substituted python's print(True) output 'True' into a JSON context, producing invalid JSON) was fixed in PR #37's cycle script (the file being committed) by adding json.dumps() for the audit heredoc path while keeping the original print() form for the shell-side drift check. This preserves the verified-correct shell comparison from the option3-cycle-script-template memory while emitting JSON-valid output. Follow-up issue #36 tracks the same fix in the other two recent cycle scripts (merge_pr{33,34}_cycle.sh). The cycle ran cleanly: drift check passed, no false positive.
